entry.title: Re: Improving SAP HANA DB Performance: Two Common CDS View Pitfalls and How to Fix Them
entry.content:
@HarshPatelV 

You are right, but worth adding one nuance: plan cache validation in development is only reliable when done against representative data volumes.
On small datasets, HANA may still select HEX - the engine switch happens under production load, which is exactly when nobody is looking at the plan cache.
That is what makes this pattern particularly difficult to catch proactively.
The broader point stands: execution plan review should be part of the definition of done for CDS development, not a post-incident step.

entry.title: Can the reconciliation / payable account be determined by transaction origin (SD vs FI) for a single
entry.content:
**Title:**
Can the reconciliation / payable account be determined by transaction origin (SD vs FI) for a single Business Partner in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

**Body:**

We are migrating from SAP ECC to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

In our current ECC landscape, the same external counterparty is maintained as **two separate master records** — one used through the SD process flow and one posted to directly from FI. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition we plan to consolidate them into a **single Business Partner**.

(For clarity: this Business Partner is set up as a *customer-supplier* — it is both invoiced through SD-driven flows and posted to directly in FI, and we owe a liability to it.)

Our accounting requirement is that the **payable / reconciliation account differs depending on where the posting originates**:

- Liability generated from an **SD-driven** process → **trade accounts payable**
- Liability posted **directly in FI** → **other (non-trade) payable**

We are already aware of the following standard constraints in Public Edition:
- *Alternative reconciliation accounts* (SSCUI 102631) only take effect for manual FI invoice apps where the reconciliation account field is set ready-for-input; they are not applied to automatic SD-driven postings.
- *Special G/L indicators* are pre-defined by SAP and cannot be newly created in Public Edition.

**Questions**
1. For one and the same Business Partner, is it possible to have the reconciliation account determined automatically based on the originating module (SD vs FI)?
2. Given the constraints above, is there any standard approach in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to achieve origin-based determination at all?
3. If standard cannot cover it, does it require custom development? Under Public Cloud extensibility (BAdI / key-user / developer extensibility), which option — if any — would apply, or is splitting into two Business Partners the only viable path?

System: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (latest active release)
Tags: `S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition`, `FI`, `Business Partner`, `Reconciliation Account`

entry.title: ERS-generated payables from a Purchase Order — can the reconciliation account be influenced (alterna
entry.content:
**Title:**
ERS-generated payables from a Purchase Order — can the reconciliation account be influenced (alternative / non-trade payable) for the same supplier in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

**Body:**

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, when we settle a Purchase Order via **ERS (Evaluated Receipt Settlement)**, the supplier invoice is posted **automatically** from the PO and goods-receipt data, with no manual line-item interaction.

Because there is no manual posting step, there appears to be **no opportunity to choose an alternative reconciliation account** — unlike the manual Supplier Invoice apps (e.g. FB60 / F0859), where the reconciliation account can be made ready-for-input and changed at posting time. As a result, the ERS-generated payable always posts to the reconciliation account taken from the supplier (Business Partner) master.

However, for the **same supplier**, we have business cases requiring **both** postings:
- some liabilities to **trade payable**
- others to **other / non-trade payable**

**Questions**
1. When a payable is created automatically via ERS, is there any standard way to influence or override the reconciliation account, or is the master-data reconciliation account always enforced?
2. For a single supplier needing both trade payable and non-trade payable, what is the recommended standard approach in Public Edition? Is creating a second supplier Business Partner (running ERS only on the trade-payable one) effectively the only viable option?
3. Are there any alternatives we are missing under Public Cloud constraints (e.g. special G/L handling via Automatic Account Determination, or handling the non-trade portion through a separate manual process)?

System: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (latest active release)
Tags: `S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition`, `MM`, `ERS`, `Reconciliation Account`, `FI-MM Integration`

entry.title: Re: Custom report : Stock In‑Out & Balance Report with Tr...
entry.content:
Hello @Grace_Li1

Thanks for the follow-up.

There is no standard transaction currency MAP calculation in inventory valuation. The proposed periodic weighted average can be created only as a custom management report. Use released stock CDS views for opening quantity, material document CDS views for movement quantities, and PO data for transaction currency values where you can. A calculated measure in Custom Analytical Queries can calculate the result at runtime, but it will not store the calculated value. For persistence, use a reporting layer such as SAP Datasphere or another supported custom reporting approach.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Simulator — Practice SPRO and FI/SD trans...
entry.content:
Hi FranciscoIacono ,
Tcode FCV3, UKM007 does not exist in S4HANA system. FCV3 is relevant to Classic Credit Management and exist only in ERP systems, but not in S/4HANA. UKM007 is a message, not a t-code.
Tcode UKM_DISPUTE also does not exist, additionally anything with 'dispute' belongs to Dispute management.
Regards,
Emöke, SAP

Re: Dangerous goods Custom Attributes
entry.content:
Hello

trying to read between the lines:

Regarding: 3. What is benefit of using additional T.,codes of Substance and specification mgmt over some custom fields in DGP* txns

By the use of the DG process only (using all of the relevant TCODes) you need to maintain DG data by material number

Depending on the number of relevant material numbers: this is not an easy story Any anyhow: you need then to prepare any phrase as needed by yourself

The Best Practise from my point is therefore: you CG02 to prepare the needed data and do DG filling

E.g. a REAL_Sub can have between 1 and noramly not more than 100 materials By using the DG Filng process: any relevant phrase is generated automically and you need to to prepare the data by cobination of material number and DG regulation

C.B

entry.title: Blog#2: How Cross Landscape Distribution (XLD) can be integrated into SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
In my first blog, I shared how pony brings custom fields to SAP Cloud ALM. As promised, this one is about the next topic: Cross Landscape Distribution (XLD).

If you need some more details about the general concept of XLD, please take a look at this very good blog of a SAP colleague which describes the functionality in Focused Build. The concept in SAP Cloud ALM has not really changed, so I will focus in this blog more about the technical integration into SAP Cloud ALM.

 

XLD directly inside the Feature 

In standard SAP Cloud ALM, there is no Cross Landscape Distribution on the Feature. You have to leave SAP Cloud ALM, run the distribution somewhere else, come back, and update the Feature status manually. With pony, XLD becomes part of the Feature process itself.

When we open a Feature in SAP Cloud ALM, we find a custom Cross Landscape Distribution area directly on the screen. There, we see:

  • The XLD status: Has the XLD already been executed, is the XLD mandatory  (the so called "Strict" mode) or optional
  • The source system, where the transport originally comes from
  • The target system, where it should be distributed to

So far, this is only visibility. The more interesting part is what happens when someone tries to move the Feature forward without executing the XLD.

 

A consistency check that protects your landscape

We added a consistency check on the Feature status. When a user tries to set the status to "Successfully Tested" (status can be configured) without running the XLD distribution first, an error message (PopUp) appears directly on the screen: "XLD distribution must be completed before setting status to 'Successfully Tested'".

XLD consistency check

 

Distribute the transport without leaving SAP Cloud ALM

To execute the XLD, the user clicks the "Distribute Transport" button in the XLD area. A popup opens inside SAP Cloud ALM with a Guided Procedure:

  • Step 1: All transports linked to the selected Feature are listed
  • Step 2: Add details, for example a description
  • Step 3: Source and target system are shown based on the configuration
  • Step 4: Select the target Features with open transports the XLD should be assigned to
  • Step 5: Summary of the distribution

After a click on "Execute", the distribution gets executed. After refreshing the Feature, we see, that the distribution was executed successfully. Now the status can be changed to "Successfully Tested", because the XLD has actually been done.

XLD Execution

Meet the XLD app

The XLD area inside the Feature is only one side of the story. The other side is the XLD app, available directly from the SAP Cloud ALM Launchpad.

In the app you will find:

  • A dashboard with helpful details on your distributions
  • All connected landscapes with executed distributions, history, and what exactly was distributed
  • Rules, which define what must be transported via XLD. These rules also drive whether "XLD required" is shown on a Feature or not. Rules can be defined per package, object type, namespace, or custom group.
  • An Analytics area that quickly shows the state of your XLD transports
  • Inbound Transports, where you can bring external transports in and integrate them via XLD into your systems
  • An audit trail over all executed XLDs and over everything that happens technically in the background

XLD App

 

A short demo video of the XLD App:

 

How it is configured, without code

All of this is set up with a graphical editor. No developer is required. How the XLD logic behaves, is defined in pony workflows. Our "XLD mandatory" workflow has:

  • A trigger on the status change in the Feature
  • An XLD status check, whether the XLD has been executed
  • A check that blocks the status change and raises an error to the user, when the XLD is still missing

 

In summary: with pony you can run the entire Cross Landscape Distribution process directly inside SAP Cloud ALM. The XLD area in the Feature, the standalone XLD app, and the graphical workflow editor work together to make XLD a real part of the Feature process. No tool switching. No manual transport coordination. No Feature status that does not reflect reality.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Thanks for reading. If you are familiar with XLD in FB and have requirements that were not covered in FB, drop it in the comments. Let's discuss how we can make it possible in SAP Cloud ALM!

In my next post, I will share how mandatory field checks are used and configured with pony for SAP Cloud ALM.

entry.title: Re: HR Trigger functionality is not automatically removin...
entry.content: Since user locking and user group changes are working, the HR Trigger configuration is likely correct. The issue is usually that the Termination Request is not generating or provisioning role-removal actions. Check the termination request details, BRF+ rules, MSMP workflow configuration, provisioning logs, and connector settings to confirm that role-removal line items are being created and sent to the target system. The most common cause is a termination process configured for account locking only rather than full access removal.

entry.title: Re: Improving SAP HANA DB Performance: Two Common CDS View Pitfalls and How to Fix Them
entry.content:
@shais 

Good point, and the post has been updated to reflect exactly this.
For Fix #1, the system-wide parameter hex_enable_with_parameters_locale is actually a prerequisite - not an alternative.
The hint at the view level is only needed on top of it for complex queries or high data volumes.
For Fix #2, FDA is already enabled by default on ABAP kernel >= 7.45, so the hint in the example is per-statement control, not feature activation.
Thanks for pushing on this - it led to a more accurate description of the flow.

entry.title: Re: Supplier Down payment adjustments and tracking
entry.content:
Hi Vagula,

To address your requirement, we need to split it into two parts: tracking the purchasing side and tracking the subsequent sale to the customer.

1. Tracking the Purchasing Side (PO to Supplier Invoice) Yes, there is an app designed specifically for this phase. You can use the Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments app (App ID F2877). This app provides a comprehensive overview of the PO item, the associated down payment requests, the cleared down payments, and the final supplier invoices.

However, to make this work, the end-to-end traceability must not be broken during the bank loan posting. If your finance team processes the advance payment purely in Finance (FI) without a Purchase Order (e.g., creating it manually in the Manage Supplier Down Payment Requests app or posting it directly from a bank statement), the linkage to the logistics process is permanently lost. An FI-only posting records the supplier and the financial amount, but it lacks the material number, plant, and quantity. Without this data, tracking the product later is impossible.

To maintain full traceability, you must strictly follow the PO-based down payment process:

  • Maintain the down payment parameters at the header/item level using the Manage Purchase Orders or Create Purchase Order - Advanced app. Please note: Simply adding the expected down payment to the PO does not automatically create the financial document needed for payment. It merely acts as a commercial agreement.

  • You must actively generate the down payment request using the Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments app.

  • This Down Payment Request is posted with a Special G/L indicator F. It is created as a Noted Item, meaning it is a single-sided entry that does not immediately update the General Ledger balances. It only appears on the supplier balance to be picked up by the payment process.

  • Once the actual payment is made (or cleared), it converts to a regular Special G/L item (e.g., indicator 'A'), updating the G/L.

  • Finally, clear the down payment upon the receipt of the actual supplier invoice.

How does this work with a Bank Loan (Third-Party Payment)? Since the bank pays the supplier directly (and not from your standard house bank account via the Manage Automatic Payments app), the clearing of that Noted Item requires a manual FI step. Your finance team will need to use the Clear Outgoing Payments app (App ID F1367) or Post with Clearing to manually clear the open Down Payment Request against the bank loan liability account. This creates the actual Down Payment document linked to the PO while recognizing the debt to the bank.

You can review the standard process in the SAP Help documentation: Down Payment Processing or here Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments 

2. Tracing the Flow to the End Customer There is no single out-of-the-box app that acts as a standalone report tracing a supplier down payment all the way to a customer invoice. In standard inventory management (Make-to-Stock), Supply and Demand are decoupled, meaning once the goods enter your general stock, the system does not link a specific supplier invoice to a specific customer invoice.

However, full visual traceability is natively available via the "Process Flow" (Document Flow) feature if you use specific account-assigned logistics scenarios:

  • Third-Party Processing (Scope Items BDK / BD3): The Sales Order automatically generates a Purchase Requisition, which becomes the PO.

  • Make-to-Order (Scope Item BJE) or Account Assigned POs: The PO is directly pegged to the Customer Sales Order.

If you use these scenarios, you can open the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app, navigate to the specific Sales Order, and click on the Process Flow tab. The system will visually map the entire chain.

In case you have some specific process in place, please describe it in detail in the comment so I can actually react to your specific business scenario.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patc...
entry.content:
Hi Andreas,

Based on what you've described, the symptoms strongly point to file locking by another process rather than a corrupted upgrade package. The fact that errors decreased between SP05 P4 and SP05 P5 attempts (but didn't disappear) and that the system actually works after the "failed" upgrade reinforces this.

Here are the most likely causes and what I'd suggest checking, roughly in order of probability:

1. Microsoft Defender is still scanning despite exclusions

Adding folders to Defender's exclusion list via the GUI sometimes doesn't apply to all engines (e.g., real-time protection, controlled folder access, ASR rules, cloud-delivered protection, tamper protection). On Windows Server 2022, Defender is particularly aggressive.

What to try:

  • Verify exclusions with PowerShell: Get-MpPreference | Select-Object ExclusionPath, ExclusionProcess, ExclusionExtension
  • Add process-level exclusions, not just paths:
    • setup.exe, BusinessObjectsServer.exe, boe_setup.exe, msiexec.exe, cscript.exe, java.exe, wdeploy*.exe
  • Also exclude:
    • D:\SAP BusinessObjects\ (whole tree)
    • D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\tmp\
    • D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\InstallCache\
    • The temp folder used by the installer (%TEMP%, %LOCALAPPDATA%\Temp, C:\Windows\Temp)
    • The folder containing the upgrade media
  • Temporarily disable real-time protection (Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $true) for the duration of the upgrade. This is the most reliable test — if it succeeds, Defender is the culprit.
  • Check if Controlled Folder Access or ASR rules are enabled (Get-MpPreference | fl *Asr*, *Controlled*) and disable them temporarily.

2. Residual CrowdStrike components

Even after uninstalling CrowdStrike Falcon, the kernel-mode sensor driver (CSAgent.sys, csagent) can sometimes remain loaded until reboot, or be only partially uninstalled if uninstall protection was enabled.

What to check:

Get-Service csagent -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
sc.exe query csagent
Get-ChildItem "C:\Windows\System32\drivers\CSAgent.sys" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

If anything is found, ensure a complete removal (you may need the maintenance token from CrowdStrike) and reboot before re-attempting the upgrade.

3. Windows Search / Indexing service

The Windows Search indexer can lock files in subfolders. Exclude D:\SAP BusinessObjects\ from indexing, or stop the Windows Search service during the upgrade:

Stop-Service WSearch

4. VSS / Backup agents

If the VM has a backup agent (Veeam, Commvault, Azure Backup, etc.) or is part of a VMware/Hyper-V scheduled snapshot, VSS writers can briefly lock files. Check if a backup window overlapped your upgrade window. Disable the backup job during the upgrade.

5. The \\?\ long-path prefix in the error

The error Access to file denied: \\?\D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\tmp\... is interesting because the \\?\ prefix means the installer is using long-path APIs. Combined with FileAccessDeniedException from Poco, this is almost always another process holding a handle, not a real ACL issue.

You can confirm with Process Monitor (Sysinternals) during the next attempt:

  • Filter on Path containing InstallData\tmp and Result = ACCESS DENIED or SHARING VIOLATION
  • The "Process Name" column will tell you exactly which process is locking the file (Defender's MsMpEng.exe, an EDR agent, the indexer, a backup agent, etc.)

This is the single most useful diagnostic step I would recommend — it will give you a definitive answer rather than guessing.

6. Other suggestions

  • Disconnect the VM from the network during the upgrade (after taking your snapshot). This prevents Defender cloud-delivered protection and any centrally pushed EDR policy from re-activating mid-install.
  • Reboot immediately before running the upgrade so no stale handles exist.
  • Stop the SIA service manually before launching setup (the installer should do this, but doing it explicitly avoids race conditions).
  • Make sure no Tomcat/Java processes are still running after the SIA stops (Get-Process java, tomcat*).
  • Check Windows Event Viewer (Application + Defender Operational log) at the timestamps in ErrorsAndWarnings.log — Defender often logs the exact file it quarantined or blocked.

About the "successful failure"

What you observed (the system works fine after the upgrade despite the error message) is consistent with the errors being on InstallCache copy operations, which don't always affect the runtime if the actual product binaries were deployed correctly. However, don't trust this — InstallCache is used for future patching/repair, and a corrupted cache will cause the next patch to fail in unpredictable ways. You did the right thing reverting.

Recommended plan

  1. Snapshot the VM.
  2. Reboot.
  3. Verify CrowdStrike is fully gone (services, drivers, files).
  4. Disable Defender real-time protection, ASR, and Controlled Folder Access via PowerShell.
  5. Stop Windows Search and any backup agent.
  6. Disconnect the network.
  7. Start Process Monitor with a filter for ACCESS DENIED.
  8. Run the upgrade.
  9. If it still fails, ProcMon will tell you exactly which process is responsible.

If after all this it still fails with no third-party process visible in ProcMon, then it becomes worth re-engaging SAP support with the ProcMon trace as evidence — at that point the problem is genuinely inside the installer.

entry.title: Re: Rework order
entry.content: Configure QM follow‑up actions so that rework orders are automatically created and reference the parent production order. Use order type RWR (or equivalent) for rework, and ensure the parent order number is captured in the reference field. This setup allows you to track rework quantities, costs, and inspection results back to the original production order.

entry.title: Guiding your customer through the new API policy and ODP-RFC
entry.content:
The way customers connect to SAP systems is changing, and you can feel it in almost every integration discussion.

This session, “Guiding your customer through the new API policy and ODP-RFC”, helps you make sense of that shift and turn it into something practical for your day-to-day work.

SAP is refining how data is accessed to improve security and consistency, especially as AI and automation become part of more solutions. Plus, topics like ODP-RFC scope and non-SAP access are getting clearer boundaries.

Honestly, the thing is, many customers will not notice the policy changes first. They will notice integration behavior, rate limits, or new constraints. That is where your guidance matters.

We will cover governed data access, SAP Business Data Cloud for non-SAP scenarios, and the April 26, 2026 API policy. You will also see how Agent2Agent fits into modern integration patterns and how to plan migrations step by step.

If you are in consulting, sales, or architecture, this session gives you a clear way to support customer conversations with confidence.

Register now and join the discussion. https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/may/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014315.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPIntegration #DataGovernance

entry.title: Re: stock removal not happening while doing MIGO because...
entry.content:
This scenario should not occur, the flag cannot be set/unset if

  • Stocks already exist
  • Stocks already exist at plant level
  • Stocks already exist for the sales order
  • Value-only materials
  • SD documents exists
  • Batches already exist
  • WM stocks (quants) already exist

Read this KBA 3225497 - FAQ: How-to change batch management flag when error message M3530 appears

  • Use the described scenarios to restore normal operations.
  • Execute an inventory clearance, potentially reverting to the prior status, followed by status adjustment and stock reposting.

entry.title: Re: copying dylib into AppBundle and licensing
entry.content:
You cannot include a Developer Edition component in an AppBundle and "put it out".  

The Developer Edition License Agreement addresses this specific question. Here are excerpts

NOTE: ALTHOUGH SQL ANYWHERE DEVELOPER EDITION MAY BE AVAILABLE AT NO CHARGE, THE PROGRAM IS NOT TRANSFERABLE. THEREFORE, YOU AGREE NOT TO DISTRIBUTE, SUBLICENSE, OR OTHERWISE TRANSFER THE PROGRAM TO (OR ALLOW THE USE OF THE PROGRAM BY) ANY THIRD PARTY.

2. Unbundling. As noted above, SAP Sybase SQL Anywhere Developer Edition is a suite of several component software products. Except as otherwise specifically authorized below, use of all components of this Program is limited to the same development Seat(s) (e.g., PC, single user workstation, or real time device) for which you acquired this Program license, and the components of this Program may not be unbundled and used by different or additional Seat(s).

3. Use of Programs.
a. Developer Use of Programs. Each copy of this SQL Anywhere Developer Edition is licensed solely for internal evaluation, development and testing of applications compatible with the Program. ...

c. No Other Rights Granted. Deployment licenses for any other purpose (including, without limitation, usage in a general production environment or on a webserver) must be obtained from Sybase before you deploy or make any other use of SQL Anywhere Developer Edition or any component thereof. You may not transfer or sublicense the Program or any component thereof to any third parties.

entry.title: Re: IMS LTI integration
entry.content:
Hi Krishna,

Short answer: SuccessFactors Learning doesn't support LTI natively. The feature has been requested but hasn't been developed yet (see this thread for SAP's own confirmation: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-q-a/succesfactors-learning-support-lti/qaq-p/12064590). Your vote on the open influence.sap.com idea might help get it prioritized.

The workaround that works in practice is to publish the LTI content as a SCORM 1.2 package, since SCORM is what SF launches natively. We do that LTI-to-SCORM bridging for our clients, so the content runs inside SF with SSO and completion tracking back in Learning. More detail here: https://linqur.com/en/lti-sap-successfactors

The approach sounds a bit roundabout, but the end result is your LTI content running inside SuccessFactors, and it can be set up in minutes. 

(Disclosure: I work at Linqur.)

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: ABAP New Era, Get to know SAP Champions and Enhancements in our platform!
entry.content:
June is bringing exciting innovations, community updates, and plenty of opportunities to connect and learn across the SAP ecosystem. In this edition, we highlight some of the latest developments for ABAP developers following the announcements at SAP Sapphire 2026, practical guidance from SAP Community experts, and new SAP Community enhancements designed to improve your experience on the platform.  

We also celebrate the introduction of the New SAP Champions, share all you need to know to participate in the latest Ask Me Anything session, and invite you to join upcoming community events taking place around the world.

Check now!

Featured Blog Posts 

Entering the New Era of Agentic AI for ABAP Development 

Following the SAP Sapphire 2026 announcement around ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code, SAP's Head of ABAP Platform, Sonja Liénard, published a blog post sharing more details about the broader vision behind these innovations. The post highlights a series of major announcements that mark what SAP describes as the most significant step forward in ABAP AI since the introduction of SAP Joule for Developers. Among the key updates are the general availability of the ABAP MCP Server, opening ABAP development to agentic AI assistants and coding agents, the upcoming release of ABAP Development Tools for VS Code, new AI-powered custom code migration agents for SAP S/4HANA transformations, expanded availability of ABAP AI capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customers, and a new consumption-based commercial model. Together, these announcements reflect SAP's strategy to make ABAP development more intelligent, accessible, and integrated with the growing AI ecosystem, while helping developers focus more on business value and less on repetitive tasks. 

Read Sonja Blog Post here 

The Future of ABAP: VS Code ADT, Zero-Config MCP, and AI Co-pilots 

Building on the announcements highlighted above, SAP Community member Lukshmi Podile takes a closer look at one of the most significant updates for ABAP developers: the general availability of ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code. In her blog post, Lukshmi explores how the new solution goes beyond being an alternative to Eclipse by introducing a modern, file-based architecture powered by the ABAP Language Server and the ABAP MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server, enabling AI-assisted and agentic development scenarios. The post explains how developers can use VS Code to work with RAP and ABAP Cloud objects, run debugging, ABAP Unit Tests, and ATC checks, while also connecting AI copilots such as SAP Joule for Developers and GitHub Copilot directly to their ABAP development environment. In addition to covering the key capabilities, Lukshmi provides a complete step-by-step setup guide to help developers get started with this new development experience. 

Read Lukshmi Blog Post here 

Controlling Excel Exports in SAP Analytics Cloud  

One of the most viewed and liked blog posts recently published in the Technology Blog Posts area comes from SAP Community member Sebastian Bock. Using a fun and relatable "Excel-Ernst" storyline, Sebastian walks through the different ways SAP Analytics Cloud users may attempt to export data to Excel and, more importantly, how administrators can control and restrict those options. The blog covers everything from disabling story and widget exports to managing Data Analyzer access, Excel Add-In usage, and Datasphere permissions. Beyond the technical settings, it also highlights an important governance principle: the strongest protection is thoughtful dashboard design that limits exposure of sensitive data from the start. Based on the engagement and positive feedback in the comments, this practical guide clearly resonated with many of our members. If you have not read it yet, we highly recommend checking it out. 

Read Sebastian Blog Post here 

Meet our New SAP Champions 

Last month, we officially welcomed 10 outstanding experts to the SAP Champions program. We are excited to see what they will bring to our SAP Community and grateful for the warm support you have shown while welcoming them across our groups and social channels. Being an SAP Champion means taking an active role in supporting community members, helping them get the most out of their SAP Community experience, sharing insights and expertise through blog posts, answering questions, contributing to discussions, and leading community-driven initiatives. To help everyone get to know our new Champions better, the Head of SAP Champions Program Mynyna Chau has published a series of blog posts in the SAP Community Leaders Finder group, introducing each of them individually. With this content, you can learn more about their areas of expertise, daily work, motivations, and what inspired them to become SAP Champions. Plus, at the end of each blog post, you will find a question from the featured Champion to the community. If you have an answer, be sure to join the conversation and share your thoughts in the comments! 

Read the New SAP Champions Blog Posts here 

Community News 

SAP Community Enhancements 

Over the past few months, our team has been working on a series of updates designed to improve the SAP Community experience. From better content discovery and navigation to clearer visibility of new activity, here are some of the latest enhancements now available: 

Clearer Profile Navigation 

  • “My Account” is now labeled “My SAP Profile” 
  • “My content” is now labeled “My Community Profile” 

These changes make it easier to distinguish between your SAP account settings and your community activity. 

Improved Search Experience 

  • The experience previously known as “Beta search” is now officially called AI Search (SAP’s Intelligent Search) 
  • AI Search is now the default search experience in public areas 
  • You can still switch between search options using the dropdown 

This update helps you find more relevant content faster and with improved accuracy. 

Smarter Content Discovery 

  • The Recent Activity feed on the homepage now defaults to “Most Commented” 
  • This makes it easier to quickly find active and engaging discussions 

Consistent “Like” Experience 

  • Continued updates replace “Kudos” with “Likes” across the platform 
  • Fixes improve consistency in search, notifications, and page labeling 

Improved Events Experience 

  • Event times in the calendar month view are now displayed correctly 
  • Times are consistent across views and reflect your browser time zone 

Better Notification Emails 

  • Fixed issues with unsubscribe links in certain emails such as badge notifications and group invitations 
  • You can now reliably manage your notification settings directly from these emails 

UI and Layout Improvements 

  • Improved spacing around Subscribe buttons for better usability 
  • Additional small layout fixes improve readability and consistency 

Performance Enhancements 

  • Optimized the floating indicator to reduce background processing 
  • This improves page speed and responsiveness 

Platform Update 

  • Upgrade to Khoros Communities 26.2, bringing general stability and performance improvements 

What all this means for you?

  • Easier navigation between SAP profile and community profile 
  • Faster, more relevant search results 
  • More engaging and timely content in feeds 
  • Clearer and more consistent terminology 
  • Improved reliability in events and notifications 
  • Better overall performance 

As always, please make sure to try out these updates and give us your feedback!  

SAP Champion Ask Me Anything – Session 4 

As we have highlighted in previous editions, the Ask Me Anything series with our SAP Champions is a fantastic opportunity to learn directly from experienced SAP experts and receive deeper, more detailed answers than what is usually possible through comments alone. Rather than a quick reply in a discussion thread, participants receive thoughtful responses through dedicated video answers, giving you a more complete view of the expert's knowledge. This month, the spotlight is on SAP Champion Sergio Guerrero, an SAP HANA and SAP Fiori specialist, author of two books covering Fiori applications and microservices in XSA, and an active contributor to SAP community events such as SAP Inside Track, CodeJams, Stammtisch gathering. Sergio's AMA session is dedicated to SAP Fiori topics, including SAPUI5, Fiori applications on HANA XSA, CAP, and S/4HANA. Community members can submit their questions directly in the comments of his blog post here or anonymously through the Forms here until June 30, 2026. After the submission period closes, Sergio will record video responses addressing the selected questions. Whether you have a specific technical challenge, want to learn from his years of experience in Fiori development and HANA modeling, or simply want to say hello and connect, this is a great chance to engage. 

Events 

SAP Charlotte Innovation Showcase 2026 

This month SAP Community Charlotte is excited to host the SAP Charlotte Innovation Showcase 2026 on June 19, a virtual event open to everyone, focused on SAP solutions, SAP BTP architectures, AI-enabled use cases, and real-world transformation stories. The format includes up to 10 speakers, selected on a first-come, first-served basis, with each session consisting of a 15-minute presentation followed by a 5-minute Q&A. Topics can cover SAP BTP, S/4HANA, AI, Event-Driven Architecture, Integration Suite, Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, industry solutions, and more. The organizers are currently looking for speakers and invite SAP architects, consultants, developers, and industry experts to submit their proposals. To apply, participants need to provide a title, a short abstract, speaker bio, SAP technologies used, and industry focus (Speakers Application Forms Here). Presenters will have the opportunity to showcase their innovations to the community, receive feedback from a panel of expert judges, and compete for the SAP Charlotte Innovator Award, with certificates provided to all speakers. If you have an interesting SAP project, implementation, architecture, or transformation story to share, this is a fantastic opportunity to get involved. Community members who prefer to attend can also register to learn from the sessions, support the speakers, and connect with fellow SAP professionals (Participation Registration Forms Here). 

Lisbon Summer Meetup 

Are you around Lisbon this month? Because there is an incredible opportunity to take part in the second edition of the SAP Community Meetup Lisbon. This Summer Edition will take place on June 18, 2026, from 6:30 PM to 8:00 PM CEST at Jardim Maria José Moura in Lisbon, Portugal. The event is for community members that are looking to expand their network, and exchange ideas in a relaxed and inspiring summer atmosphere. This informal gathering encourages participants to share experiences, discuss innovation, learn from one another, and strengthen connections within the local SAP ecosystem. Whether you are already active in the community or looking to meet fellow members for the first time, this is a great chance to engage with the SAP Community in Portugal! Register now here. 

SAP CodeJam and SAP Inside Track in Weinheim 

If you are already planning your SAP Community activities for September, consider joining two community events in Weinheim, Germany! On September 18, 2026, SAP Developer Advocate DJ Adams (@qmacro) will host SAP CodeJam focused on CAP CDS modelling at the Syntax Auditorium in Weinheim (register for the CodeJam here). The organizers are also hosting an informal community get-together on that evening for speakers, attendees, and anyone already in town. On the following day, September 19, the first SAP Inside Track Weinheim will take place at the same venue, for a full day of community-led sessions, networking, and knowledge sharing (register for the Inside Track here). The organizers are currently looking for speakers for the SIT (register to be a Speaker at the SIT here). and community members are invited to submit sessions on a wide range of SAP topics. First-time speakers are especially encouraged to participate, with session lengths available in both 20- and 40-minute formats. Please make sure to read the Event Organizer Max Streifeneder full blog post here for more details.

entry.title: Overtime Calculation when employee records time on 6th and 7th day
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a customer requirement that the employee is paid overtime for hours worked beyond 40 hours in a week. However, if the employee recorded a time on the 6th and 7th day, they should automatically be considered overtime. 

For example, the employee recorded time (regardless of if it is rostered day or not) on Monday, Wed-Sunday - then Sunday is the 6th day so whatever the hours entered on that day will be considered overtime, regardless is the total number of hours worked for that week is less than 40 hours.

If the employee worked Wednesday- Sunday, he only worked for 5 days. Therefore, no OT payment for 6th or 7th day.

This is a bit tricky because we can't just use Saturday and Sunday as filters to fetch the overtime hours, but it has to be the 6th and 7th day worked.

We tried to use counted events but it's not consistently fetching the 6th and 7th day.

Any ideas, how can we achieve this requirement?

entry.title: SAP Subscription Billing × S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 連携（57Z）— 前提知識と全体アーキテクチャ
entry.content:
はじめに

近年、製造業をはじめとする多くの業界でサブスクリプションビジネスへの転換が加速しています。たとえば、産業機械メーカーが従来の「機械本体の単品販売」に加え、以下のような複合的な収益モデルを展開するケースが増えています。

課金種別内容例在庫管理処理システム
一回限り
（One-time）		機械本体の販売50万円/台あり
（在庫品）		S/4HANA Cloud Sales
一回限り
（One-time）		セットアップ費用など
（※本シナリオ対象外）		—なし
（非在庫品）		SAP Subscription Billing
定期課金
（Recurring）		IoTサービス月額料金3万円/月なし
（非在庫品）		SAP Subscription Billing
従量課金
（Usage-based）		稼働時間に応じた加算料金500円/時間なし
（非在庫品）		SAP Subscription Billing

このようなハイブリッド課金モデルを既存のSAP S/4HANA Cloud環境に統合するための標準ソリューションが、SAP Best Practicesスコープアイテム 57Z「Subscription Management with Sales Billing」 です。

本記事では、57Z連携の概要・システムアーキテクチャ・データフローの前提知識を解説します。各機能の詳細設定手順については次回以降の記事で取り上げます。

57Z とは

57ZはSAP Subscription BillingとSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition（以降、S/4HANA Cloud）を統合するためのSAP Best Practicesスコープアイテムです。

Lead to Cashのエンドツーエンドビジネスプロセス「Solution Business for Cloud Deployment」の一部として位置づけられており、以下の3つの基本プロセスを実現します。

  1. サブスクリプションの生成 — S/4HANA CloudのAPIまたはソリューション受注からSubscription Billingにサブスクリプションを作成
  2. 使用量収集とレーティング — 従量課金・定期課金・一回限り課金の自動計算
  3. 請求データ転送 — Subscription BillingからS/4HANA CloudのSales Billingへ請求データを連携し、顧客請求書を発行

システムアーキテクチャ — 連携サービス全体像

57Z連携を構成するコンポーネントと、それぞれの役割を理解することがアーキテクチャ設計の第一歩です。ドキュメントに記載されている連携図（Integrated Data）を以下で解説します。

コンポーネントと役割

コンポーネント役割
SAP Subscription Billingサブスクリプション・請求予測・請求データの管理とレーティング
SAP Master Data Integration（MDI）S/4HANA CloudからSubscription Billingへのマスタデータ同期（推奨）
SAP Cloud Application Event Hubサブスクリプション・請求予測のイベント連携。Subscription BillingとS/4HANA Cloudが同一BTPリージョン内にある場合に使用（推奨）
SAP Event Mesh両システムが異なるBTPリージョンにある場合（例：EUとAPJ）のイベント連携代替手段（defaultプラン）
SAP Cloud Integrationマスタデータ連携・請求データ連携の旧方式（Deprecated／非推奨）
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editionソリューション受注管理・Sales Billing・財務処理

重要： SAP Cloud IntegrationのIntegration Flowによるマスタデータ連携は現在非推奨（Deprecated）です。新規プロジェクトでは MDI（マスタデータ）＋ Event Hub（イベント） の構成を採用してください。

データフローの全体像

マスタデータ連携（MDI経由）： MDIを介したマスタデータ連携には2つの方向があります。ビジネスパートナはS/4HANA CloudからSubscription Billingへ顧客として同期されます。一方、製品・料金要素はSubscription BillingからS/4HANA Cloudへ購読請求製品（SBPD）・購読請求レート要素（SBRE）として連携されます。

ソリューション受注との連携： S/4HANA CloudのSolution OrdersからSubscription Billingへサブスクリプションが作成され、逆方向にはサブスクリプションのシミュレーション結果がSolution Ordersに返されます。

トランザクションデータ連携（Event Hub経由）： Subscription Billingで生成されたサブスクリプションと請求予測が、Business EventsとしてS/4HANA Cloud側のプロバイダー契約・請求プランに反映されます。

請求データ連携（ダイレクト連携）： Subscription BillingのBillsが、S/4HANA CloudのBilling Document Requests（SBDR）としてダイレクトに連携されます。

まとめ

  • 57Zは一回限り・定期・従量の複合課金モデルをSAP標準で実現するスコープアイテム
  • マスタデータ同期にはMDI、イベント連携にはEvent Hubを使用するのが現在の推奨構成
  • SAP Cloud IntegrationのIntegration Flowは非推奨であり、新規実装では使用しない
  • Event HubとEvent Meshの選択はBTPリージョンの構成に依存する

次回以降の記事では、以下の各プロセスの詳細設定手順を順番に解説していきます。

  1. ビジネスパートナ登録（MDIによるマスタデータ連携）
  2. 料金要素登録（Rate Element）
  3. 料金プランテンプレート登録
  4. 製品登録（Product Configuration in Subscription Billing）
  5. サブスクリプション登録・請求（Sales Billingへのデータ転送）

entry.title: Re: Cannot migrate AuC during fiscal year
entry.content:
Hi Paaa,

The errors FAA_POST 504 ("Cumul. values not allowed as asset value date is in transfer year") and AA 322 ("Value date is earlier than capitalization date") occur due to standard Legacy Data Transfer (LDT) validation logic in SAP Asset Accounting.

Your transfer date is April 30, 2026, which makes 2026 your current transfer year. You are attempting to migrate values for AUC001 and AUC002 with capitalization/value dates in January and February 2026 using the "Cumulative Values" structure.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Cumulative Values are strictly reserved for historical asset balances accumulated up to the end of the last closed fiscal year (e.g., December 31, 2025). Any costs incurred between the start of the transfer year and the mid-year transfer date (January 1, 2026 – April 30, 2026) are classified as Current Year Acquisitions (or transactions during the year of transfer) and cannot be migrated as cumulative legacy balances.

Technical Solution

To resolve this issue within the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit (app "Migrate Your Data"):

  1. Clear Cumulative Fields: Remove all data entries for AUC001 and AUC002 from the Cumulative Values sheet in your XML migration template.

  2. Use Transaction Sheets: Move the migration data for these two AuCs to the Transaction Header and Transaction Items sheets.

  3. Map Dates Correctly: Populate the amounts as current year transactions. Ensure the Asset Value Date used in the transaction item exactly matches or is later than the Capitalization Date (e.g., 01/06/2026 for AUC001).

Please check these notes describing your issue: 3309477 and Transfer During the Fiscal Year 

Best Regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: Production order change log.
entry.content:
Good day @sriny1081 

Thank you for your question.

In the Public Cloud, there is no standard production order change history report that shows all changes such as changed yield quantity, component quantity, added or removed components, User ID, date, time, etc. See KBA 3664613 for more details.

In simple terms:

  • There is no standard report in Public Cloud for full production order change logs.
  • The Display Changes functionality does not provide production order change history in the way you require.
  • IMG based activation or SAP Note 390635 is an on-premise or private cloud type approach, not a Public Cloud configuration option.
  • Order Change Management is not something you can activate step by step in Public Cloud through IMG.
  • You may consider raising an improvement request.

 

The Public Cloud only offers the possibility to check available order information through apps such as
Manage Production Orders, 

Display Production Order, Monitor Production Order or Process Orders

  

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: issue in useful life calculation and the depreciation calculation
entry.content:
We have a ROU asset which we have put to use from 1st March, but the acquisition posting is done on 1st June 2026. 

Now when we see the deprciation calculation we can see that the March month depreciation is not calculated even though we have maintained March 1st as the depn start date and the capitalization date. The system is calculating the depn from April 1st and taking the useful life as 35 months instead of 36 months.

I am attaching the calculation of the same . Kindly help us understand this issue

entry.title: SharePoint Knowledge Base in SAP Service Cloud v2 (aka V2)
entry.content:
Hello,

For those with some experience with the use of MS SharePoint as a knowledge base (KB) in V2, please assist:

We have enabled integration to SharePoint (via CPI), but we have the following issues/concerns/questions:

1. Some icons (on the KB panel) are disabled/non-responsive:
- likes
- email

2. A Microsoft login is required to view the retrieved documents in V2. Is this expected behavior? If it is, we would want to eliminate this step for the users. When we copy the document link and paste it into the browser (outside of V2), we can view the document content.

3. I see we could display number of views (for each document) on the KB panel. Is this possible for the SharePoint documents, how can it be achieved?

4. We can preview some document types in V2, within the agent desktop/KB panel (images and PDFs - after MS login), inconsistently though, the preview doesn't always load. Anything we need to look into here? Are previews limited to specific document types, what is that list?

entry.title: Re: Is LLM fine-tuning really dead?
entry.content:
@Marla-Ellinger 

Fine-tuning isn’t really dead, but it is definitely not the default answer for every problem anymore. In many cases, good prompting, retrieval, and context engineering can get you further with less effort and less complexity. That said, fine-tuning still makes sense when you need very consistent structured outputs, a specialized domain behavior, or a smaller model that has to run efficiently. The real question is not whether fine-tuning is dead — it is whether it is the right tool for the specific job.

entry.title: Re: Improve Your Scrum Process with AI
entry.content:
@_Dimitri_ 

AI can make Scrum a lot smoother, but only if it is used to support the team rather than replace the team’s judgment. The biggest value I see is in reducing the manual work around summaries, backlog cleanup, blocker detection, and sprint reporting so the team can spend more time on real collaboration. When used well, AI does not change the purpose of Scrum — it simply removes some of the noise around it.

entry.title: Re: how can i check doubled posted and doubled cleared it...
entry.content:
To identify duplicate entries, consider sorting the FBL5N transaction or Manage Customer Line Items app by the following key fields:

  • Company Code
  • Customer Number
  • Currency
  • Reference Number
  • Amount in Document Currency
  • Document Date

(Note: This method is more commonly used to detect duplicate vendor invoices.)

There are some Customizing options to trigger check at input, look for 3340510 - Duplicate Invoice Check overview / how-to scenarios
